name: dufflebag
title: dufflebag

commands.name: dufflebag
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# dufflebag -h
 Usage: dufflebag AWS_REGION
 Example: dufflebag us-east-1
 Dufflebag can only operate in one AWS region at a time
 If you want to search every region, you'll have to deploy several instances.
 See also /usr/share/doc/dufflebag/README.md

binary_metadata.file_type: Bourne-Again shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.depends: golang-any, golang-github-aws-aws-sdk-go-dev, golang-github-deckarep-golang-set-dev, golang-lukechampine-blake3-dev (>= 1.1.5), make, sensible-utils, zip
apt_metadata.description:
Search exposed EBS volumes for secrets (program)
Dufflebag is a tool that searches through public Elastic Block Storage (EBS)
snapshots for secrets that may have been accidentally left in.
.
The tool is organized as an Elastic Beanstalk ("EB", not to be confused
with EBS) application, and definitely won't work if you try to run it
on your own machine.
.
Dufflebag has a lot of moving pieces because it's fairly nontrivial
to actually read EBS volumes in practice. You have to be in an AWS
environment, clone the snapshot, make a volume from the snapshot, attach
the volume, mount the volume, etc... This is why it's made as an Elastic
Beanstalk app, so it can automagically scale up or down however much you
like, and so that the whole thing can be easily torn down when you're
done with it.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dufflebag because ### dufflebag.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dufflebag AWS_REGION` as documented upstream for dufflebag.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dufflebag with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dufflebag --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### dufflebag.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install dufflebag
Install with: sudo apt install dufflebag
